meta.title: BMW 520d Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 520d engine — M47, N47 and B47. E60, F10, G30 and G60 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 520d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 520d engine typically costs £3,200–£5,500 supply only depending on generation, with the B47 currently sitting at £3,400–£6,200. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

It depends on the generation. Early E60 cars with the M47 are rated ★★★★☆ (robust, with EGR/swirl flap issues). Pre-2016 cars with the N47 have the well-documented rear-chain risk (★★★☆☆). 2016+ cars with the B47 have the chain relocated to the front (★★★★☆), eliminating the specific N47 failure mode.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 520d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain wear on N47-engined cars (pre-2016), EGR cooler coolant leaks affecting both N47 and B47, and M47 swirl flap failure on early E60 cars. The B47 is significantly more reliable than the N47.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 520d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well-kept F10 520d worth £6,000–£10,000, a £4,200 recon is 42–70% of the car's value — assess carefully. For a G30 worth £12,000+, the same recon is 35% — more justifiable. Always assess the rest of the car before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 520d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E60 2003–2008 is M47, E60 late 2008–2010 and F10 2010–2015/16 is N47, 2015/16-onward is B47. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£3,200–£5,500 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

rows.eraNote: The 520d's early years — M47 was robust but EGR and swirl flap issues; late 2008 N47 introduced the rear-chain risk.
The core 520d generation — N47 chain risk applies to early cars; 2015/16 facelift brought the B47 with front-mounted chain.
The modern 520d — B47 with front-mounted chain; 48V mild-hybrid on later cars.
The latest 520d — further revised B47 with 48V mild-hybrid; the most refined 520d yet.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 520d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before it becomes catastrophic
Straightforward fix if caught before overheating
If caught early — far cheaper than a new engine
Blanking plates remove the risk entirely
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 520d? Check These First
buyIf: * 2016+ B47 if you want to avoid the N47 chain risk entirely
* Documented timing-chain history if buying a pre-2016 N47
* Confirmed EGR recall completion on any 520d
* Full service history with sub-15,000km oil intervals
avoidIf: * Pre-2016 N47 with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* EGR recall not completed on any 520d
* Poor service history on any generation — the 5 Series is unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis, regardless of generation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 520d is the core 5 Series diesel — and the engine choice determines your ownership experience. 2016+ B47 cars are meaningfully more reliable than the earlier N47. Buy on documented history, and if you're looking at a pre-2016 car, demand chain-history proof before you commit.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 520d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (timing chain warning on N47)
* Service History — Including which engine era (M47 for pre-2008, N47 for 2008–2016, B47 for 2016+) — see the Era Map above
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding?
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.label: Most Common Failure
